questions.question: Explain the key differences between Android and iOS mobile operating system security architectures. Discuss how each platform implements application sandboxing and permission models to protect user data.
keyPoints: Android uses SELinux mandatory access control and sandboxing via Linux kernel user permissions
iOS implements closed-source security with hardware-backed keychain and Secure Enclave
Android permission model: runtime permissions (Android 6.0+), signature-level permissions
iOS uses entitlement-based access control and App Store vetting process

questions.question: Define Mobile Device Management (MDM) and list four essential security features that an enterprise MDM solution should provide for securing corporate mobile devices.
keyPoints: MDM is a framework for managing, monitoring, and securing mobile devices in enterprise environments
Key features: remote wipe/lock, encryption enforcement, policy compliance enforcement
Application management: whitelisting/blacklisting, containerization (MDM/MAM)
Network security: VPN configuration, Wi-Fi management, certificate-based authentication

questions.question: A banking organization plans to deploy mobile banking applications for its employees and customers. (a) Discuss the mobile security architecture required to protect sensitive financial data, including secure boot, trusted execution environment, and API security measures. (b) Explain the OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities that pose the greatest risk to mobile banking apps and propose mitigation strategies for at least three of them.
keyPoints: Secure boot chain: hardware root of trust → bootloader → OS kernel → applications
TEE/SE for cryptographic operations: isolated execution environment for sensitive operations
API Security: mutual TLS authentication, certificate pinning, input validation, rate limiting
OWASP M1: Improper Credential Usage - implement secure storage (Android Keystore/iOS Keychain)
OWASP M2: Inadequate Supply Chain Security - code signing verification, app integrity checks
OWASP M3: Insecure Authentication/Authorization - biometric integration, JWT token security with refresh tokens

questions.question: With reference to regulatory compliance frameworks in India, discuss the legal and regulatory requirements for securing mobile devices that process personal and sensitive data. Also, explain the enterprise mobile security lifecycle management approach including device procurement, enrollment, policy enforcement, and retirement phases.
keyPoints: Regulatory compliance: DPDP Act 2023 provisions for personal data processing on mobile devices
RBI guidelines for mobile banking security: two-factor authentication, transaction limits, device registration
NIST-based mobile security framework adapted for Indian enterprise context
Device procurement: hardware security module integration, UEM/EMM solution selection
Enrollment: identity verification, certificate-based authentication, containerization setup
Policy enforcement: encryption standards (AES-256), password policies, jailbreak/root detection
Retirement: remote wipe, data sanitization verification, de-provisioning from MDM console
